note: Nebraska block = package with high transitive reach relative to direct dependency visibility. Thresholds scaled per ecosystem size and dependency model.

npm.character: Individual open-source authors (sindresorhus, jonschlinkert). Utility micro-packages.

go.character: Go core team (Russ Cox, Ian Lance Taylor, Dmitri Shuralyov). Tutorial packages that escaped into production.

top_packages.name: bindex
top_packages.note: Stack frame binding - pulled in by better_errors

top_packages.name: jekyll-swiss
top_packages.note: Jekyll theme pulled by GitHub Pages

top_packages.name: jekyll-theme-cayman
top_packages.note: Jekyll theme pulled by GitHub Pages

rubygems.character: Jekyll/GitHub Pages gem chain. Individual maintainers holding gems with 0-18 stars but 300-550K dependent repos. Highest peak amplification of any non-Actions ecosystem (145,628x).

top_packages.name: winapi-x86_64-pc-windows-gnu
top_packages.note: Windows x64 API bindings helper crate

top_packages.name: winapi-i686-pc-windows-gnu
top_packages.note: Windows x86 API bindings helper crate

top_packages.name: proc-macro-error-attr
top_packages.note: Proc macro error helper - 14 stars, 35K repos

cargo.character: Platform target crates (winapi-*, windows_*) by a single maintainer (retep998) holding Windows ecosystem. Proc macro helper crates with tiny star counts but massive reach.

top_packages.name: django-heroku
top_packages.note: Deprecated Heroku config helper - 0 dependent packages

top_packages.name: itypes
top_packages.note: Basic type objects for REST frameworks

top_packages.name: wsgiref
top_packages.note: Python stdlib WSGI reference - packaged separately on PyPI

pypi.character: Deprecated deployment packages (django-heroku, django-toolbelt) that Heroku's tutorials spread into production. Standard library modules re-packaged (wsgiref).

top_packages.name: com.facebook.react:hermes-android
top_packages.note: Hermes JS engine for React Native Android

maven.character: React Native (Facebook/Meta) and Spring Boot (Pivotal/VMware) sub-artifacts. Lower amplification than other ecosystems — Maven's explicit POM declarations compress ratios.

packagist.character: Sebastian Bergmann (PHPUnit author) is the PHP sindresorhus. Multiple sebastian/* packages with 400-500K dependent repos, each with 1 maintainer. The entire PHP testing ecosystem routes through one person.

top_packages.name: actions/checkout
top_packages.note: Used by virtually every GitHub Actions workflow

actions.character: Special case: the actions ecosystem has virtually no action-to-action dependencies, so amplification equals dependent_repos_count directly. GitHub's own actions/* org controls the most critical actions. Peak amp of 1,334,666x is an artifact of the flat dependency model, not vulnerability.

pub.character: Flutter/Dart SDK internals. Google's own SDK packages (sky_engine, dart-lang org, flutter org) dominate — the same pattern as Go, where the language team's packages are load-bearing infrastructure. Maintainer count is 0 for all (Google-internal authorship not exposed via pub.dev).

top_packages.name: Folly
top_packages.note: Facebook's C++ utility library - pulled by React Native

cocoapods.character: React Native (Facebook/Meta) dragging in C++ dependencies. Folly and Flipper are Facebook's internal tools that every React Native iOS app installs. Flipper-PeerTalk: 1 star, 35,000 repos.

top_packages.name: coverlet.collector
top_packages.note: Code coverage - pulled by dotnet test template

nuget.character: Lowest peak amplification of any ecosystem (1,201x). .NET's NuGet has stricter explicit dependency declaration. Microsoft packages and dotnet test tooling dominate.

top_packages.name: punycode
top_packages.note: IDN domain encoding - mathias bynens (also in npm)

bower.character: Deprecated (Bower was sunset in 2017). The Nebraska layer is frozen — nobody is removing these dependencies because nothing is actively maintained. mathiasbynens appears here too (same as npm).

top_packages.name: matplotlib
top_packages.note: Most widely depended-on data viz library

top_packages.name: pytest-cov
top_packages.note: Coverage plugin pulled by pytest template

top_packages.name: pip
top_packages.note: pip inside conda — package manager inception

conda.character: Data science toolchain. matplotlib and pip show up here as transitive deps of nearly every conda environment. pip inside conda (780x) is the clearest example of package manager inception.

top_packages.name: phoenix_live_reload
top_packages.note: Phoenix Framework's live reload dev dependency

hex.character: Benoît Chesneau (benoitc) is the Elixir sindresorhus — 4 packages in the top 5, each with 1 maintainer. The Erlang/Elixir HTTP and networking stack routes through one person.

hackage.character: Very low amplification (max 368x) and small absolute numbers — Haskell's ecosystem is small and explicit. Testing infrastructure (hspec, tasty) dominates.

top_packages.name: org.clojure/clojure
top_packages.note: The Clojure runtime itself — every Clojure project depends on it

clojars.character: org.clojure/clojure is the language runtime — it shows up as a Nebraska block because projects depend on it directly but list almost no transitive packages. Ring HTTP stack dominates the rest.

cran.note: R has no Nebraska layer. Max amplification across all CRAN packages is ~45x. R's DESCRIPTION file convention — strict Depends/Imports fields enforced by CRAN policy — means every dependency is explicitly declared. The amplification ratio stays flat by design.

who_holds_it.go: Go core team (Russ Cox, Ian Lance Taylor, Dmitri Shuralyov)
who_holds_it.rubygems: Individual gem authors + GitHub Pages toolchain
who_holds_it.cargo: Single maintainer (retep998) owns Windows platform target crates
who_holds_it.actions: GitHub itself (actions/* org) — structurally different risk profile
who_holds_it.pub: Google/Flutter SDK team (dart-lang org, flutter org)
who_holds_it.cocoapods: Facebook/Meta (React Native iOS dependencies: Folly, Flipper)
who_holds_it.clojars: Clojure core team + Ring HTTP library
